question_text: Which statement correctly explains why the Moon orbits the Earth?

options.label: A
options.text: Gravity from the Earth pulls the Moon towards it, and the Moon's sideways motion keeps it in orbit instead of falling straight down

options.label: B
options.text: The Moon orbits because it is moving away from the Earth at high speed

options.label: C
options.text: There is no gravity acting on the Moon, so it moves in a straight line around the Earth

options.label: D
options.text: The Moon's own gravity is stronger than Earth's gravity, so it pulls itself along in orbit

correct_answer: A) Gravity from the Earth pulls the Moon towards it, and the Moon's sideways motion keeps it in orbit instead of falling straight down
explanation: Gravity constantly pulls the Moon towards Earth, but because the Moon is also moving sideways at high speed, this creates a curved path around Earth. Without gravity, the Moon would move in a straight line; without sideways motion, it would fall straight down. The combination creates a stable orbit.
